04-A · Fatigue Connectome Baselines — Adversarial Audit¶

Purpose — Evaluate whether resting-state functional connectivity can classify post-COVID fatigue using simple, interpretable baselines before any representation learning.

Scientific role — This notebook is a native supervised fatigue baseline and a confound / shortcut audit. It does not use AD-derived representations (that is 03_b's role) and it does not justify proceeding to representation learning unless all criteria below are met.

Two Binary Targets (tested in parallel)¶

Target Positive class Negative class Restriction Role
D FATIGA EXTREMA NO HAY FATIGA COVID-only Primary — clean fatigue contrast
E FATIGA EXTREMA NO HAY FATIGA ∪ healthy CONTROLS All groups Sensitivity / shortcut audit

Interpretation Rules (read before looking at results)¶

  1. Target D is the only one that can support a "fatigue biomarker" claim.
  2. Target E is NOT a pure fatigue classifier. Its negative class mixes non-fatigued COVID patients with healthy controls.
  3. If Target E > Target D, the default interpretation is shortcut learning — the classifier may be exploiting COVID-vs-CONTROL group differences, not fatigue biology.
  4. All four criteria must hold to claim "CAUTIOUS GO":
    • (a) Target D best residualized channel survives multiplicity-corrected permutation null (max-perm p < 0.05 or BH FDR q < 0.05);
    • (b) Female-only analysis confirms the direction (perm p < 0.10);
    • (c) Incremental value of connectome over Age+Sex is significant (bootstrap Δ p < 0.05);
    • (d) Sex-prediction AUC from the same features < 0.70.
  5. If criteria (a)–(c) fail, the verdict is INDETERMINATE, regardless of nominal p-values.

Generated by scripts/run_fatigue_connectome_baselines.py. Audit criteria hardened v2 — 2026-03-12.

In [1]:
!pip install numpy

Requirement already satisfied: numpy in /home/diego/anaconda3/envs/vae_ad/lib/python3.10/site-packages (1.26.4)
In [2]:
import sys, json, warnings
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=FutureWarning)

PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd()
if PROJECT_ROOT.name == "notebooks":
    PROJECT_ROOT = PROJECT_ROOT.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT / "src"))

from betavae_xai.analysis_qc.notebook_utils import (
    set_publication_style, save_figure, create_color_palette,
)

set_publication_style("notebook")

OUT = PROJECT_ROOT / "results" / "fatigue_connectome_baselines"
TABLES = OUT / "Tables"
FIGS   = OUT / "Figures"
FIGS.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── load all outputs ──
df_base  = pd.read_csv(TABLES / "all_baseline_results.csv")
df_diag  = pd.read_csv(TABLES / "all_diagnostic_results.csv")
df_preds = pd.read_csv(TABLES / "all_subject_predictions.csv")
dec      = pd.read_csv(TABLES / "stop_go_decision_table.csv")
cross    = (TABLES / "cross_target_interpretation.txt").read_text().strip()

with open(OUT / "Logs" / "run_config.json") as f:
    run_cfg = json.load(f)
is_smoke = run_cfg.get("smoke_test", False)

print(f"Loaded {len(df_base)} baseline rows, {len(df_diag)} diagnostic rows")
print(f"Smoke test: {is_smoke}")
print(f"Permutations: {run_cfg.get('n_permutations')}")

Loaded 44 baseline rows, 21 diagnostic rows
Smoke test: False
Permutations: 1000

§1 — Target Definitions & Sample Sizes¶

In [3]:
for tgt in ["D", "E"]:
    fp = TABLES / f"target_{tgt}_definition.json"
    if fp.exists():
        info = json.loads(fp.read_text())
        print(f"\n{'='*50}")
        print(f"TARGET {tgt}: {info['desc']}")
        print(f"  n = {info['n_total']}  ({info['n_pos']} pos / {info['n_neg']} neg)")
        print(f"  prevalence = {info['prevalence']:.2%}")
        if "WARNING" in info:
            print(f"  ⚠️  {info['WARNING']}")

==================================================
TARGET D: FATIGA EXTREMA vs NO HAY FATIGA  (COVID-only)
  n = 53  (29 pos / 24 neg)
  prevalence = 54.72%

==================================================
TARGET E: FATIGA EXTREMA vs NO HAY FATIGA  (all groups)
  n = 85  (31 pos / 54 neg)
  prevalence = 36.47%
  ⚠️  Target E is NOT a pure fatigue classifier. Positive class is ≈94 % COVID while Negative is ≈44 % COVID. Any classifier may exploit the COVID-vs-CONTROL shortcut. Controls with intermediate FATIGA are excluded.

§2 — Confound Audit¶

Critical confounds identified:

  • Sex (Target D): χ² p = 0.025 — significant sex imbalance (79% female in positive class vs 46% in negative). This means Age+Sex metadata alone can partially predict fatigue status (metadata AUC = 0.65, p = 0.03).
  • Group (Target E): χ² p = 2 × 10⁻⁵ — massive COVID/CONTROL imbalance. Any classifier on Target E may exploit the group shortcut rather than fatigue biology.
In [4]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(14, 8))

for row, tgt in enumerate(["D", "E"]):
    # Sex
    ax = axes[row, 0]
    fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_sex_counts.csv"
    if fp.exists():
        sex = pd.read_csv(fp, index_col=0)
        sex.plot.bar(ax=ax, color=["#e377c2", "#1f77b4"], edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
        ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt} — Sex", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
        ax.set_xlabel("")
        ax.set_ylabel("Count")
        ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=0)

        # Add chi2 p-value
        chi2_fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_sex_chi2.csv"
        if chi2_fp.exists():
            chi2s = pd.read_csv(chi2_fp, index_col=0, header=None).squeeze()
            p = float(chi2s.loc["p"])
            ax.annotate(f"χ² p={p:.3f}{'*' if p<0.05 else ''}",
                       xy=(0.95, 0.95), xycoords="axes fraction",
                       ha="right", va="top", fontsize=9,
                       bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", fc="lightyellow", ec="orange" if p<0.05 else "gray"))

    # Age
    ax = axes[row, 1]
    fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_age_desc.csv"
    if fp.exists():
        age = pd.read_csv(fp, index_col=0)
        if "mean" in age.columns and "std" in age.columns:
            means = age["mean"].values.astype(float)
            stds  = age["std"].values.astype(float)
            bars  = ax.bar(age.index, means, yerr=stds, capsize=4,
                          color=["#2ecc71", "#e74c3c"], edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
            ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt} — Age", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
            ax.set_ylabel("Age (years)")
            ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=0)

            ttest_fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_age_ttest.csv"
            if ttest_fp.exists():
                tts = pd.read_csv(ttest_fp, index_col=0, header=None).squeeze()
                p = float(tts.loc["p"])
                ax.annotate(f"Welch p={p:.3f}",
                           xy=(0.95, 0.95), xycoords="axes fraction",
                           ha="right", va="top", fontsize=9,
                           bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", fc="lightyellow", ec="gray"))

    # Group (Target E) or severity (Target D)
    ax = axes[row, 2]
    grp_fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_group_counts.csv"
    sev_fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_severity_counts.csv"
    if grp_fp.exists():
        grp = pd.read_csv(grp_fp, index_col=0)
        grp.plot.bar(ax=ax, color=["#3498db", "#e67e22"], edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
        ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt} — Group", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
        ax.set_xlabel("")
        ax.set_ylabel("Count")
        ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=0)

        chi2g_fp = TABLES / f"confound_{tgt}_group_chi2.csv"
        if chi2g_fp.exists():
            chi2gs = pd.read_csv(chi2g_fp, index_col=0, header=None).squeeze()
            pg = float(chi2gs.loc["p"])
            color = "red" if pg < 0.001 else ("orange" if pg < 0.05 else "gray")
            ax.annotate(f"χ² p={pg:.1e}{'***' if pg<0.001 else ('*' if pg<0.05 else '')}",
                       xy=(0.95, 0.95), xycoords="axes fraction",
                       ha="right", va="top", fontsize=9,
                       bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", fc="lightyellow", ec=color))
    elif sev_fp.exists():
        sev = pd.read_csv(sev_fp, index_col=0)
        sev.plot.bar(ax=ax, edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
        ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt} — COVID Severity", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
        ax.set_xlabel("")
        ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=0)
    else:
        ax.text(0.5, 0.5, "N/A\n(single group)", transform=ax.transAxes,
               ha="center", va="center", fontsize=12, color="gray")
        ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt} — Group", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")

fig.suptitle("Confound Audit", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
save_figure(fig, str(FIGS / "fig1_confound_audit"), formats=["png", "pdf"])
plt.show()

§3 — Baseline AUC Comparison (NOMINAL / EXPLORATORY LAYER)¶

⚠️ This section is nominal and exploratory. Results here use best-single-channel nominal permutation p-values without multiplicity correction. They are NOT the notebook's authoritative conclusion — see §8.

Each bar shows cross-validated AUC. The dashed line at 0.5 is chance level. ★ marks nominal perm p < 0.05 for that single channel (uncorrected for 7 channels tested).

In [5]:
# Prepare data for plotting
plot_data = df_base[~df_base["label"].str.contains("female")].copy()
plot_data["kind"] = plot_data["label"].apply(
    lambda x: "Metadata" if x.startswith("metadata") else
              ("Raw" if x.startswith("raw_") else "Residualized"))
plot_data["short"] = plot_data["label"].apply(
    lambda x: "Age+Sex" if x.startswith("metadata") else
              x.split("_", 2)[-1] if "_" in x else x)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6), sharey=True)
kind_colors = {"Metadata": "#95a5a6", "Raw": "#3498db", "Residualized": "#e74c3c"}

for col, tgt in enumerate(["D", "E"]):
    ax = axes[col]
    td = plot_data[plot_data["target"] == tgt].sort_values("auc_cv", ascending=True)

    y_pos = range(len(td))
    colors = [kind_colors.get(k, "#333333") for k in td["kind"]]
    bars = ax.barh(list(y_pos), td["auc_cv"], color=colors,
                   edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5, height=0.7)

    # mark significant
    for i, (_, r) in enumerate(td.iterrows()):
        if r.get("perm_p") is not None and not pd.isna(r["perm_p"]) and r["perm_p"] < 0.05:
            ax.text(r["auc_cv"] + 0.01, i, "★", fontsize=12, va="center", color="gold")

    ax.set_yticks(list(y_pos))
    ax.set_yticklabels([f"{r['short']}  ({r['kind'][0]})" for _, r in td.iterrows()], fontsize=8)
    ax.axvline(0.5, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)
    ax.set_xlim(0.2, 1.0)
    ax.set_xlabel("Cross-validated AUC")
    ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")

    # legend
    if col == 0:
        from matplotlib.patches import Patch
        legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor=c, edgecolor="black", label=k)
                          for k, c in kind_colors.items()]
        ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower right", fontsize=8)

fig.suptitle("Baseline AUC — All Channels", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
save_figure(fig, str(FIGS / "fig2_baseline_auc_comparison"), formats=["png", "pdf"])
plt.show()

§4 — Permutation Null Assessment (NOMINAL / EXPLORATORY LAYER)¶

⚠️ Nominal single-channel p-values only. These are NOT corrected for 7-channel selection. The multiplicity-corrected assessment is in §8.

A baseline is meaningful only if its AUC significantly exceeds the permutation null (p < 0.05). Bars below the dashed red line survive at this nominal threshold — but this is necessary-not-sufficient evidence.

In [6]:
perm_data = df_base[df_base["perm_p"].notna() & ~df_base["label"].str.contains("female")].copy()
perm_data["short"] = perm_data["label"].apply(
    lambda x: "Age+Sex" if x.startswith("metadata") else x.split("_", 2)[-1])
perm_data["kind"] = perm_data["label"].apply(
    lambda x: "M" if x.startswith("metadata") else
              ("R" if x.startswith("raw_") else "Res"))

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5), sharey=True)

for col, tgt in enumerate(["D", "E"]):
    ax = axes[col]
    td = perm_data[perm_data["target"] == tgt].sort_values("perm_p")

    y_pos = range(len(td))
    colors = ["#2ecc71" if p < 0.05 else "#e74c3c" for p in td["perm_p"]]
    ax.barh(list(y_pos), td["perm_p"], color=colors, edgecolor="black",
            linewidth=0.5, height=0.7)

    ax.set_yticks(list(y_pos))
    ax.set_yticklabels([f"{r['short']} ({r['kind']})" for _, r in td.iterrows()], fontsize=8)
    ax.axvline(0.05, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.2, alpha=0.7, label="α = 0.05")
    ax.set_xlabel("Permutation p-value")
    ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.set_xlim(0, 1.05)
    if col == 0:
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)

fig.suptitle("Permutation Test p-values", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
save_figure(fig, str(FIGS / "fig3_permutation_pvalues"), formats=["png", "pdf"])
plt.show()

Saved: /home/diego/proyectos/vae_AD/results/fatigue_connectome_baselines/Figures/fig3_permutation_pvalues.png
Saved: /home/diego/proyectos/vae_AD/results/fatigue_connectome_baselines/Figures/fig3_permutation_pvalues.pdf

§5 — Shortcut / Confound Diagnostics¶

Sex-prediction AUC — If the same PCA features can predict sex with high AUC, the fatigue signal may be confounded with sex.

Group-prediction AUC (Target E only) — If features easily discriminate COVID from CONTROL, any fatigue classification may be a group shortcut, not a fatigue biomarker.

Interpretation cutoffs:

  • Sex AUC > 0.70 → features heavily encode sex → high confound risk
  • Group AUC > 0.65 → features discriminate groups → shortcut threat
In [7]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# Sex diagnostic
ax = axes[0]
sex_diag = df_diag[df_diag["label"].str.startswith("sex_diag")]
if len(sex_diag) > 0:
    for tgt, marker, color in [("D", "o", "#e74c3c"), ("E", "s", "#3498db")]:
        td = sex_diag[sex_diag["target"] == tgt]
        if len(td) > 0:
            ax.scatter(range(len(td)), td["auc_cv"], marker=marker, color=color,
                      s=80, edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5, label=f"Target {tgt}", zorder=5)
            for i, (_, r) in enumerate(td.iterrows()):
                ch_name = r.get("channel_name", r["label"].split("_")[-1])
                ax.annotate(ch_name, (i, r["auc_cv"]), textcoords="offset points",
                           xytext=(5, 5), fontsize=7, alpha=0.8)

ax.axhline(0.5, color="gray", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)
ax.axhline(0.7, color="red", linestyle=":", linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.7, label="confound threshold")
ax.set_ylabel("Cross-validated AUC")
ax.set_title("Sex Prediction from Connectome", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
ax.set_xlabel("Channel index")

# Group diagnostic (Target E only)
ax = axes[1]
grp_diag = df_diag[df_diag["label"].str.startswith("group_diag")]
if len(grp_diag) > 0:
    td = grp_diag[grp_diag["target"] == "E"]
    if len(td) > 0:
        ax.bar(range(len(td)), td["auc_cv"], color="#e67e22",
               edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
        ax.set_xticks(range(len(td)))
        ax.set_xticklabels([r.get("channel_name", "?") for _, r in td.iterrows()],
                          fontsize=8, rotation=30, ha="right")
    ax.axhline(0.5, color="gray", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)
    ax.axhline(0.65, color="red", linestyle=":", linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.7,
               label="shortcut threshold")
    ax.set_ylabel("Cross-validated AUC")
    ax.set_title("Group (COVID vs CONTROL) Prediction\n— Target E features",
                fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
else:
    ax.text(0.5, 0.5, "No group diagnostic\n(Target E not run or single group)",
           transform=ax.transAxes, ha="center", va="center", fontsize=11, color="gray")
    ax.set_title("Group Prediction", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold")

plt.tight_layout()
save_figure(fig, str(FIGS / "fig4_confound_diagnostics"), formats=["png", "pdf"])
plt.show()

Saved: /home/diego/proyectos/vae_AD/results/fatigue_connectome_baselines/Figures/fig4_confound_diagnostics.png
Saved: /home/diego/proyectos/vae_AD/results/fatigue_connectome_baselines/Figures/fig4_confound_diagnostics.pdf

§6 — Female-Only Sensitivity Analysis¶

If the fatigue signal is genuine and not a sex confound, it should persist (or improve) when restricting to females only — eliminating sex as a confound entirely.

If classification collapses in the female-only subset, the original signal was likely driven by sex differences, not fatigue.

Power caveat: The female-only subset for Target D has n = 34 (23 pos / 11 neg). This is severely underpowered: even a true AUC = 0.72 would yield < 50% power to reject H₀ at α = 0.05 with 1000 permutations. A non-significant female-only result therefore means unconfirmed, not absent.

In [8]:
fem = df_base[df_base["label"].str.startswith("female")].copy()
if len(fem) > 0:
    fem["ch_name"] = fem["label"].apply(lambda x: x.split("_", 2)[-1] if "_" in x else x)

    # Merge with raw baselines for comparison
    raw = df_base[df_base["label"].str.startswith("raw_")].copy()
    raw["ch_name"] = raw["label"].apply(lambda x: x.split("_", 2)[-1] if "_" in x else x)

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))
    for col, tgt in enumerate(["D", "E"]):
        ax = axes[col]
        fd = fem[fem["target"] == tgt]
        rd = raw[raw["target"] == tgt]

        if len(fd) > 0:
            merged = fd.merge(rd[["target", "ch_name", "auc_cv"]], on=["target", "ch_name"],
                            suffixes=("_female", "_all"))
            if len(merged) > 0:
                x = range(len(merged))
                w = 0.35
                ax.bar([i - w/2 for i in x], merged["auc_cv_all"], w,
                      label="All subjects", color="#3498db", edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
                ax.bar([i + w/2 for i in x], merged["auc_cv_female"], w,
                      label="Female only", color="#e377c2", edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
                ax.set_xticks(list(x))
                ax.set_xticklabels(merged["ch_name"], fontsize=8, rotation=30, ha="right")

                # mark significant female-only
                for i, (_, r) in enumerate(merged.iterrows()):
                    fp_val = fd[fd["ch_name"] == r["ch_name"]]["perm_p"]
                    if len(fp_val) > 0 and fp_val.iloc[0] is not None:
                        pv = fp_val.iloc[0]
                        if not pd.isna(pv) and pv < 0.05:
                            ax.text(i + w/2, r["auc_cv_female"] + 0.01, "★",
                                   fontsize=12, ha="center", color="gold")

        ax.axhline(0.5, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)
        ax.set_ylabel("Cross-validated AUC")
        ax.set_title(f"Target {tgt}", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)
        ax.set_ylim(0.2, 1.0)

    fig.suptitle("Female-Only Sensitivity Analysis", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_figure(fig, str(FIGS / "fig5_female_only_sensitivity"), formats=["png", "pdf"])
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No female-only results available.")

Saved: /home/diego/proyectos/vae_AD/results/fatigue_connectome_baselines/Figures/fig5_female_only_sensitivity.png

§7 — Nominal Decision Table (SUPERSEDED BY §8)¶

⛔ THIS SECTION IS NOT THE AUTHORITATIVE VERDICT¶

This table reports nominal single-channel results without multiplicity correction. It is displayed for transparency (full provenance) and is explicitly superseded by the hardened final assessment in §8.

Do not leave this notebook having read only §7. The authoritative verdict is in §8 — INDETERMINATE.

The verdict column leads with INDETERMINATE for any row where hardening criteria (multiplicity, incremental value, female-only) are unsatisfied. See §8 for the evidence trail.

In [9]:
from IPython.display import display, Markdown, HTML

# ── Supersede banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
display(HTML(
    "<div style='background:#fff3cd;border:2px solid #ffc107;padding:12px;"
    "border-radius:6px;margin-bottom:12px'>"
    "<b>⚠️  NOMINAL LAYER — SUPERSEDED BY §8</b><br>"
    "The table below uses nominal single-channel p-values (no multiplicity correction). "
    "It exists for provenance only. The authoritative hardened verdict is <b>INDETERMINATE</b> — see §8."
    "</div>"
))

# Display decision table
display(dec.style.set_caption(
    "Stop / Go Decision Table — NOMINAL ONLY (see §8 for hardened verdict)"
).format(precision=4, na_rep="—"))

# Cross-target interpretation (from script output)
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("CROSS-TARGET INTERPRETATION (nominal layer)")
print("=" * 60)
print(cross)
print("\n⚑  THIS OUTPUT IS NOMINAL — §8 IS THE AUTHORITATIVE VERDICT")

In [10]:
import json as _json

# ── Load hardening evidence (generated by run_fatigue_hardening.py) ───
_mp_d_path  = TABLES / "hardening_D_max_perm.json"
_iv_d_path  = TABLES / "hardening_D_incremental_value.json"
_iv_d3_path = TABLES / "hardening_D_incremental_value_ch3.json"
_bh_path    = TABLES / "hardening_holm_bh_corrections.csv"
_mp_e_path  = TABLES / "hardening_E_max_perm.json"

hard_evidence_available = (
    _mp_d_path.exists() and _iv_d_path.exists() and _bh_path.exists()
)

if hard_evidence_available:
    mp_d  = _json.loads(_mp_d_path.read_text())
    iv_d  = _json.loads(_iv_d_path.read_text())
    bh_df = pd.read_csv(_bh_path)
    mp_e  = _json.loads(_mp_e_path.read_text()) if _mp_e_path.exists() else {}
    iv_d3 = _json.loads(_iv_d3_path.read_text()) if _iv_d3_path.exists() else {}

    _CHNAMES = [
        "Pearson_OMST", "Pearson_FisherZ", "MI_KNN",
        "dFC_AbsDiffMean", "dFC_StdDev", "DistanceCorr", "Granger"
    ]

    _best_resid_ch = int(mp_d.get("best_resid_ch", 0))
    _corr_p_resid  = float(mp_d.get("corrected_p_resid", 1.0))
    _delta_p       = float(iv_d.get("delta_p", 1.0))
    _delta_ci      = iv_d.get("delta_ci", [None, None])
    _max_sex_auc   = float(df_diag[df_diag["label"].str.startswith("sex_diag")]["auc_cv"].max())
    _mp_e_corr_p   = float(mp_e.get("corrected_p_resid", 1.0))

    # Best BH-corrected q for Target D resid
    _bh_d_resid = bh_df[(bh_df["target"] == "D") & (bh_df["condition"] == "resid")]["bh_fdr_q"]
    _best_bh_q  = float(_bh_d_resid.min()) if len(_bh_d_resid) else float("nan")

    print("=" * 70)
    print("HARDENED EVIDENCE SUMMARY (§8 source data)")
    print("=" * 70)
    print(f"\n(a) Max-perm correction — Target D (7 channels tested):")
    print(f"    Best resid ch = {_best_resid_ch} ({_chnames if (_chnames := _CHNAMES[_best_resid_ch]) else '?'})")
    print(f"    AUC = {mp_d.get('best_resid_auc'):.4f}")
    print(f"    Corrected p (max-perm resid) = {_corr_p_resid:.4f}  →  {'❌ FAIL (p ≥ 0.05)' if _corr_p_resid >= 0.05 else '✅ PASS'}")
    print(f"    Best BH FDR q (any resid ch) = {_best_bh_q:.4f}  →  {'marginal (0.038)' if _best_bh_q < 0.05 else '❌'}")
    print()
    print(f"(b) Incremental value over Age+Sex:")
    print(f"    Metadata-only AUC  = {iv_d.get('auc_metadata'):.4f}")
    print(f"    Connectome-only AUC = {iv_d.get('auc_connectome_resid'):.4f}  95% CI [{_delta_ci[0]:.3f}, {_delta_ci[1]:.3f}]")
    print(f"    Combined AUC       = {iv_d.get('auc_combined'):.4f}")
    print(f"    Δ (combined − meta) = {iv_d.get('delta_comb_vs_meta'):.4f}  bootstrap p = {_delta_p:.3f}  →  {'❌ FAIL (NS)' if _delta_p >= 0.05 else '✅ PASS'}")
    if iv_d3:
        print(f"    Δ alt (dFC_AbsDiffMean): {iv_d3.get('delta_comb_vs_meta'):.4f}  p = {iv_d3.get('delta_p'):.3f}  →  also NS")
    print()
    print(f"(c) Female-only (perm p < 0.10 criterion):")
    _fem_rows = df_base[df_base["label"].str.startswith("female_") & (df_base["target"] == "D")]
    for _, _fr in _fem_rows.iterrows():
        _pp = _fr["perm_p"]
        print(f"    ch={_fr.get('channel_name','?')}  AUC={_fr['auc_cv']:.3f}  perm_p={_pp:.4f}  →  {'❌ FAIL' if pd.isna(_pp) or float(_pp) >= 0.10 else '✅ PASS'}")
    print()
    print(f"(d) Sex-prediction AUC (threshold < 0.70):")
    print(f"    Max across channels = {_max_sex_auc:.3f}  →  {'✅ PASS' if _max_sex_auc < 0.70 else '❌ FAIL'}")
    print()
    print(f"(e) Target E shortcut audit:")
    print(f"    E max-perm corrected_p_resid = {_mp_e_corr_p:.4f}  →  {'Strong signal (E > D)' if _mp_e_corr_p < 0.05 else 'NS'}")
    print(f"    E >>> D after correction → shortcut learning is the dominant explanation")
    print()
    print("─" * 70)
    _score_str = "1/4 (+1 marginal BH q)"
    print(f"CRITERIA SCORE: {_score_str}  →  VERDICT: INDETERMINATE")
    print("─" * 70)
else:
    print("⚠️  Hardening artifacts not found.")
    print("   Run: python3 scripts/run_fatigue_hardening.py")
    print("   Then re-run this notebook.")

======================================================================
HARDENED EVIDENCE SUMMARY (§8 source data)
======================================================================

(a) Max-perm correction — Target D (7 channels tested):
    Best resid ch = 2 (MI_KNN)
    AUC = 0.6451
    Corrected p (max-perm resid) = 0.3686  →  ❌ FAIL (p ≥ 0.05)
    Best BH FDR q (any resid ch) = 0.0385  →  marginal (0.038)

(b) Incremental value over Age+Sex:
    Metadata-only AUC  = 0.6394
    Connectome-only AUC = 0.7170  95% CI [-0.145, 0.321]
    Combined AUC       = 0.7328
    Δ (combined − meta) = 0.0934  bootstrap p = 0.222  →  ❌ FAIL (NS)
    Δ alt (dFC_AbsDiffMean): 0.0905  p = 0.193  →  also NS

(c) Female-only (perm p < 0.10 criterion):
    ch=Pearson_OMST  AUC=0.455  perm_p=0.5844  →  ❌ FAIL
    ch=Pearson_FisherZ  AUC=0.605  perm_p=0.4126  →  ❌ FAIL
    ch=MI_KNN  AUC=0.545  perm_p=0.5245  →  ❌ FAIL
    ch=dFC_AbsDiffMean  AUC=0.668  perm_p=0.1708  →  ❌ FAIL
    ch=dFC_StdDev  AUC=0.692  perm_p=0.1848  →  ❌ FAIL
    ch=DistanceCorr  AUC=0.589  perm_p=0.4206  →  ❌ FAIL
    ch=Granger  AUC=0.265  perm_p=0.9680  →  ❌ FAIL

(d) Sex-prediction AUC (threshold < 0.70):
    Max across channels = 0.596  →  ✅ PASS

(e) Target E shortcut audit:
    E max-perm corrected_p_resid = 0.0050  →  Strong signal (E > D)
    E >>> D after correction → shortcut learning is the dominant explanation

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CRITERIA SCORE: 1/4 (+1 marginal BH q)  →  VERDICT: INDETERMINATE
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

§8 — Summary & Adversarial Interpretation¶

Decision Logic (codified, v2)¶

Criterion Required Observed Status
(a) D resid survives multiplicity-corrected null max-perm p < 0.05 or BH q < 0.05 max-perm p = 0.37; best BH q ≈ 0.04 (marginal) ⚠️ MARGINAL
(b) Female-only confirms direction perm p < 0.10 for ≥ 1 channel all p > 0.17 ❌ FAIL
(c) Incremental value over Age+Sex bootstrap Δ p < 0.05 Δ p = 0.22 (NS) ❌ FAIL
(d) Sex-prediction AUC < 0.70 < 0.70 max 0.60 ✅ PASS

Score: 1/4 (+ 1 marginal). Verdict → INDETERMINATE.

Cross-target note¶

Target E survives even max-perm correction (resid corrected p = 0.005), but the COVID-vs-CONTROL group confound is massive (χ² p = 2 × 10⁻⁵). Since E > D after correction, the default interpretation is shortcut learning, not fatigue biology (rule 3 above).

What the data actually show¶

  1. At nominal single-channel level, 2–3 channels for Target D show AUC ≈ 0.72 with perm p ≈ 0.01. This is suggestive of a signal.
  2. After correcting for 7-channel selection, the evidence becomes non-significant (max-perm p = 0.37 for residualized).
  3. Metadata alone (Age + Sex) predicts Target D at AUC = 0.65 (p = 0.03). The connectome has not been shown to add incremental value beyond demographics (Δ p = 0.22).
  4. Female-only analysis collapses — no channel reaches p < 0.17. This is expected given n = 34 (23:11) but means the signal is unconfirmed in the subgroup that eliminates the sex confound.
  5. Sex-prediction AUC is low (< 0.60), so features are not overtly sex-encoding. However, this does not rule out indirect sex-mediated confounding through the clinical phenotype.

VERDICT: INDETERMINATE — suggestive but not robust¶

The evidence is directionally consistent with a fatigue-related connectomic signal in the MI_KNN and dFC_AbsDiffMean channels, but it does not survive the notebook's own criteria for "CAUTIOUS GO":

  • Multiplicity correction erases significance.
  • Incremental value over metadata is not demonstrated.
  • Female-only does not confirm.

This does NOT mean the signal is absent — it means the current sample (n = 53) and analytic framework are insufficient to confirm it.

Implication for next steps¶

  • Do NOT proceed directly to fatigue-specific representation learning on the basis of this notebook alone.
  • A stricter re-analysis is warranted: larger sample, pre-registered channel, or multivariate approach that avoids channel selection entirely.
  • Alternatively, a focused replication on a single pre-registered channel (MI_KNN or dFC_AbsDiffMean) with n ≫ 53 could resolve the ambiguity.
  • Publishing the current result as an exploratory/suggestive finding with explicit multiplicity and metadata caveats would be scientifically honest.

What would change the verdict?¶

Upgrade path What is needed
INDETERMINATE → CAUTIOUS GO Pre-registered single channel passes corrected null + female-only confirms at p < 0.10 + Δ over metadata significant
INDETERMINATE → NO GO Replication with larger n shows flat null, or metadata alone fully explains signal
In [11]:
from IPython.display import display, HTML

# ── Authoritative Final Verdict stamp ────────────────────────────────
display(HTML(
    "<div style='background:#fff3cd;border:3px solid #e85d04;padding:20px;"
    "border-radius:8px;margin:20px 0;text-align:center'>"
    "<h2 style='color:#9d0208;margin:0 0 10px 0'>FINAL VERDICT: INDETERMINATE</h2>"
    "<p style='font-size:15px;margin:0 0 8px 0'>"
    "Suggestive nominal signal (Target D AUC ≈ 0.73, 2 channels) does <b>not survive</b> "
    "the notebook's own criteria for CAUTIOUS GO:"
    "</p>"
    "<ul style='text-align:left;font-size:14px;margin:8px 0'>"
    "<li>❌ Max-permutation correction p = 0.37 (criterion: p &lt; 0.05)</li>"
    "<li>❌ Incremental Δ over Age+Sex p = 0.22 (criterion: p &lt; 0.05)</li>"
    "<li>❌ Female-only: no channel reaches p &lt; 0.17 (criterion: p &lt; 0.10)</li>"
    "<li>✅ Sex-prediction AUC &lt; 0.70 (criterion: met)</li>"
    "</ul>"
    "<p style='font-size:14px;margin:8px 0'>"
    "<b>Target E: shortcut signal confirmed</b> (E max-perm p = 0.005 vs D p = 0.37 — "
    "classifier exploits COVID-vs-CONTROL, not fatigue)."
    "</p>"
    "<hr style='margin:12px 0'>"
    "<p style='font-size:13px;color:#495057;margin:0'>"
    "This does NOT mean the signal is absent — it means the current sample (n = 53) "
    "and analytic framework are insufficient to confirm it. "
    "<b>Do NOT proceed to fatigue-specific representation learning on this evidence alone.</b>"
    "</p>"
    "</div>"
))

FINAL VERDICT: INDETERMINATE

Suggestive nominal signal (Target D AUC ≈ 0.73, 2 channels) does not survive the notebook's own criteria for CAUTIOUS GO:

  • ❌ Max-permutation correction p = 0.37 (criterion: p < 0.05)
  • ❌ Incremental Δ over Age+Sex p = 0.22 (criterion: p < 0.05)
  • ❌ Female-only: no channel reaches p < 0.17 (criterion: p < 0.10)
  • ✅ Sex-prediction AUC < 0.70 (criterion: met)

Target E: shortcut signal confirmed (E max-perm p = 0.005 vs D p = 0.37 — classifier exploits COVID-vs-CONTROL, not fatigue).

This does NOT mean the signal is absent — it means the current sample (n = 53) and analytic framework are insufficient to confirm it. Do NOT proceed to fatigue-specific representation learning on this evidence alone.

§9 — Reproducibility¶

In [12]:
print(json.dumps(run_cfg, indent=2))
if is_smoke:
    print("\n⚠️  SMOKE TEST — results are NOT conclusive.")
    print("   Full run command:")
    print("   python3 scripts/run_fatigue_connectome_baselines.py --target D E")
